prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a very dark, nearly unreadable frame; no clear objects or people are visible.

It then cuts to a backstage or studio setting. A man is seen from behind, wearing a dark top, facing a woman. The woman stands in front of him holding a small camera up near her face, as if photographing him. The setting includes bright round studio/makeup lights, metal rigging, and a ladder, suggesting a photo-shoot or production environment.

A close-up follows of the man’s face in profile. He has dark, styled hair and is smiling or speaking. The background remains studio-like, with blurred lights and equipment.

The next shot shows a black-and-white portrait photograph of the same man. In the upper-left corner of the image, the word “Simulation” appears. The portrait looks like a printed photo or mock-up, angled within the frame.

The commercial then focuses on the woman in a medium close-up. She faces the camera directly, smiling. She has styled shoulder-length hair, earrings, and wears a dark burgundy/purple shirt. Studio equipment remains blurred in the background. In the next shot, she tilts her head slightly and holds up a white bottle at the lower left edge of the frame; the label is only partly visible here.

A close-up then shows the back of the man’s head. A hand parts or lifts his dark hair, apparently inspecting the scalp or hair. The man’s face is visible only as a blurred reflection in the background. White subtitle text at the bottom reads: “After 3 weeks of regular use.”

The final product-style shots show a tabletop arrangement. Two black-and-white photographs of the man lie on a dark surface, along with office or studio items such as a clipboard, keys, and papers. A blue banner across the upper left reads: “Great Hair Can’t Have Flakes.” In the last frame, a white bottle labeled “Head & Shoulders” appears on the right side of the tabletop, with a blue and red graphic on the label, next to the same photographs and the same “Great Hair Can’t Have Flakes” text.
